name: RABBIT-FILE-BACKLOG-7
title: item-status.py update has no length/charset validation on title and description
description: cmd_update enforces priority value set, but title and description accept arbitrary strings - no max length, no control-character stripping, no newline normalization. A user can pass a 10MB description or a title containing terminal escape codes, corrupting list-items.py output. Add reasonable bounds (e.g. title <= 200 chars, description <= 10KB) and strip control characters except whitespace.

history.note: Reopening: as-shipped implementation deviates significantly from filed spec. Audit 2026-05-19. (1) Description cap is 500 chars; original spec said 10KB — 20x tighter than intended; observed in another session blocking legitimate update with 637 and 2148 char descriptions. (2) Title and description share the same cap; spec said title<=200, description<=10KB — should be per-field. (3) Cap applies to item-status.py update only; file-item.py has NO length check — creates asymmetry where you can file a 5KB description but cannot update to anything >500 chars. (4) Control-character stripping requirement not verified post-ship. Fix: MAX_TITLE_LEN=200, MAX_DESCRIPTION_LEN=10240, apply both in file-item.py and item-status.py update, verify control-char handling. Cross-reference: BACKLOG-12 hit the same ship-vs-intent gap pattern in this 2026-05-18 housekeeping wave.
